title: Whole-Body Bone Scintigraphy Research
subtitle: Medical imaging researcher. Three years from manual annotation to a published journal paper, a clinical application, and a bachelor's thesis.
reflection: I learned to fully retrain a segmentation model (nnU-Net v2), not just fine-tune it. Setting up the full training pipeline from data format to inference is a different task.
I learned to process medical imaging data, including DICOM files, and understand what clinicians actually look at when they read a scan.
I learned to coordinate with teammates across different backgrounds: coders, doctors, thesis supervisors. Keeping the work moving is its own skill.
Annotating data manually teaches you exactly where models fail.
I contributed to building Telplastina: an application that doctors can open and use. That is different from writing a paper about it.

highlights: Assigned as lead coder on a Ministry of Higher Education-funded project, with no prior bone scan experience.

Annotated 600+ scan images manually across all skeletal regions, supervised by nuclear medicine physicians at Dr. Hasan Sadikin Hospital, Universitas Padjadjaran, working from raw scan data without automated labeling tools.
Built detection and segmentation code covering 12 anatomical regions, from hotspot localization to full skeletal mapping.
BSI quantification pipeline surpassed both EXINI and BONENAVI (the two commercial systems in clinical use at the time), measured by patient-level diagnostic accuracy.
Visited Dr. Hasan Sadikin Hospital to recalibrate BSI scoring criteria with nuclear medicine doctors. Prior criteria came from literature, not clinical practice.
Expanded the dataset to anterior and posterior views across all 12 skeletal regions.
Took the research from code into a working clinical application for the first time: built Telplastina v1 with the team.
Submitted the first preprint to SSRN, January 2025.
Consolidated two years of bone scan research into a bachelor's thesis.
Led Telplastina v2 development: full-stack clinical application with Vue 3 frontend, Rust backend, and Docker deployment.
Journal paper accepted at IJIES-INASS. Full dataset published on Zenodo and open to the public.
